dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, an alliance that took years to build has been shattered. Not by war. By betrayal from within.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Our representative was caught secretly working with the {enemy_type} to undermine our partners. It was calculated: a violation of every covenant we swore. the {quest_focus} {quest_focus_is} in ruins, and {quest_item} was stolen in the process. If someone could {quest_action}, it would prove we're willing to make it right.
dialogueChunks.outro: Without that gesture, the {quest_threat} {quest_threat_is} all we have to look forward to. The {quest_stakes} depends on whether trust can be rebuilt.

playerResponses.text: Broken trust is hard to mend, but returning what was stolen is a start.
Why should I clean up your representative's mess?
You broke the alliance. Live with the consequences.

dialogueChunks.intro: Someone in a position of sacred trust has been selling us out to the {enemy_type} for months, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three attacks in two months, all targeting us at our most vulnerable. the {quest_focus} was only discovered because a wounded scout overheard a {enemy_type} laughing about knowing exactly when our defenses change. We need someone from outside to {quest_action}. {quest_item} {location_hint} might hold the proof.
dialogueChunks.outro: If the defenders learn about this from their own ranks, it'll tear the unit apart. Hard evidence is the only way to remove the traitor without destroying the {quest_stakes}. {quest_time_pressure}.

playerResponses.text: An oath-breaker in command is worse than any enemy. I'll find the proof.
What if the proof implicates others too?

dialogueChunks.intro: The founding covenant guaranteed every family equal standing. Someone has replaced the original document with a forgery that gives one family everything, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Families received eviction notices this morning. The {quest_focus} {quest_focus_is} the legal basis. Nobody knows when the original was swapped, but the forgery is convincing. If someone can {quest_action} from {quest_origin}, or find proof it was altered, the evictions stop.
dialogueChunks.outro: The {quest_threat} starts at the end of the week. The {quest_stakes} have nowhere to go. {quest_item} {location_hint} might hold the key to proving the fraud.

playerResponses.text: Forging a community's founding document is the worst kind of theft. I'll help.
If the original is destroyed, what then?
Legal disputes aren't really my expertise.
